[Bug] Cron agent session generates duplicate reply despite --no-deliver flag
items.body:
## Summary
Cron jobs configured with `--no-deliver` still produce duplicate messages in Telegram when the agent script sends a message via `openclaw message send` AND the cron agent session generates its own reply turn.

## Version
OpenClaw 2026.4.2 (d74a122)

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a cron job with `--no-deliver` flag
2. Cron message instructs agent to run a Python script
3. The script internally calls `openclaw message send` to deliver its output to a Telegram DM
4. Observe: the script delivers the message (delivery #1), then the agent session generates its own reply summarising what the script did (delivery #2)

## Example Cron Config
```
openclaw cron add \
  --name "stale-session-check-daily" \
  --no-deliver \
  --message "Run python3 /path/to/script.py — the script handles its own Telegram delivery. Do not send any additional message or reply."
```

## Observed Behaviour
- Message received in Telegram channel **twice** at the same timestamp
- Both messages have identical content
- Occurs consistently across multiple cron jobs (stale session check, history limit monitor, daily channel summary)
- The explicit instruction "Do not send any additional message or reply" in the cron message payload is ignored

## Expected Behaviour
With `--no-deliver`, the cron agent session should suppress all outbound channel messages from its own reply turn. The agent should execute the script silently, with zero agent-generated output to any channel.

## Additional Context
- Attempted workaround: adding explicit "do not reply" instruction to cron message payload — unreliable, agent still generates a reply turn
- `--no-deliver` correctly suppresses the "announce" delivery mode but does not suppress the agent's own conversational reply
- Affects all cron jobs where the script handles its own delivery — which is the recommended pattern when per-channel targeting is needed

## Suggested Fix
`--no-deliver` should suppress ALL outbound messages from the cron agent session, including agent-generated replies. Either:
1. Add a `--silent` flag that prevents the agent from generating any reply turn at all
2. Have `--no-deliver` imply reply suppression (agent runs the task but sends no output anywhere)
3. Expose a `--reply-target none` option that explicitly routes agent replies to /dev/null

## Environment
- OS: Linux 6.12.73+deb13-amd64
- Node: v22.22.0
- Channel: Telegram
- Session target: isolated

items.title: Control UI blank screen: unresolved bare module import for markdown-it-task-lists
items.body:
## Bug

After upgrading to OpenClaw 2026.4.14, the Control UI serves a blank page on all browsers (including localhost on the Pi, incognito windows, etc.).

### Root Cause

The built JS bundle `dist/control-ui/assets/index-Dlv1J1hX.js` contains an unresolved bare ES module specifier:

```js
import N from"markdown-it-task-lists"
```

Browsers cannot resolve bare module specifiers. The `markdown-it-task-lists` package is not present in `node_modules` and was not bundled inline by Vite. This causes an `Uncaught TypeError: Failed to resolve module specifier` which prevents the entire UI from loading.

### Error in Browser Console

```
?token=...:1 Uncaught TypeError: Failed to resolve module specifier "markdown-it-task-lists". Relative references must start with either "/", "./", or "../".
```

After manually patching that import, a second error appeared because code calls `md.core.ruler.after("github-task-lists", ...)` which requires the plugin to register a core rule with that exact name.

### Workaround

1. Created a shim file at `dist/control-ui/assets/markdown-it-task-lists.js` with the full markdown-it-task-lists plugin implementation (sourced from the npm package on GitHub)
2. Patched the import to use a relative path: `import N from"./markdown-it-task-lists.js"`
3. UI loads and works correctly after this patch

### Environment
- OpenClaw: 2026.4.14
- Platform: ARM64 (Raspberry Pi, Debian bookworm)
- Node: 22.22.2
- Access: both `http://127.0.0.1:18789` and `https://mcserver1.tail773f41.ts.net`

### Note
This patch lives in the dist directory and will be overwritten on the next update. A proper fix would ensure the Vite build bundles markdown-it-task-lists inline or provides it as a proper chunk with a relative import path.
